channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: The biggest ‘Schrödinger’s cat’ yet — physicists put 7,000 atoms in superposition
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00227-2

Quantum superposition gets supersized — plus, US science after a year of Trump.
item.dc:title: The biggest ‘Schrödinger’s cat’ yet — physicists put 7,000 atoms in superposition
item.dc:creator: Nick Petrić Howe
Benjamin Thompson

item.title: Extreme barocaloric effect at dissolution
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10013-1

An extreme barocaloric effect in NH4SCN aqueous solutions is enabled by pressure-induced dissolution and precipitation, but without using a separate heat-transfer liquid.
item.dc:title: Extreme barocaloric effect at dissolution
item.dc:creator: Kun Zhang
Yifang Liu
Ying Gao
Zhe Zhang
Haoyu Wang
Wanwu Li
Xiaoyan Fan
Jiayu Ding
Ziqi Guan
Shogo Kawaguchi

Jiaqing Zhang
Lei Su
Yiming Li
Runjian Jiang
Yifan Li
Yating Jia
Yanxu Wang
Jianchao Lin
Jinlong Zhu
Peng Tong
Suxin Qian
Kuo Li
Zhidong Zhang
Bing Li

item.title: Symmetry, microscopy and spectroscopy signatures of altermagnetism
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09883-2

The symmetry, microscopy and spectroscopy signatures of altermagnetism are reviewed, and compared with traditional ferromagnetism and Néel antiferromagnetism, and magnetic phases with symmetry-protected compensated non-collinear spin orders.
item.dc:title: Symmetry, microscopy and spectroscopy signatures of altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: Tomas Jungwirth
Jairo Sinova
Rafael M. Fernandes
Qihang Liu
Hikaru Watanabe
Shuichi Murakami
Satoru Nakatsuji
Libor Šmejkal

item.title: Core–envelope miscibility in sub-Neptunes and super-Earths
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09970-4

First-principles molecular dynamics driven by density functional theory is used to show that silicate and hydrogen are completely miscible over a wide range of plausible core–envelope pressure–temperature conditions.
item.dc:title: Core–envelope miscibility in sub-Neptunes and super-Earths
item.dc:creator: Travis Gilmore
Lars Stixrude

item.title: The potential for bridgmanite megacrysts to drive magma ocean segregation
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10063-5

Large-scale molecular dynamics simulations show that the crystal–melt interfacial energies of MgSiO3 bridgmanite increase substantially with pressure, potentially forming unusually large bridgmanite crystals and suggesting a new physical mechanism driving magma ocean segregation.
item.dc:title: The potential for bridgmanite megacrysts to drive magma ocean segregation
item.dc:creator: Jie Deng
Junwei Hu
Yidi Shi
Jina Lee
Haiyang Niu
Lars Stixrude

item.title: Probing quantum mechanics with nanoparticle matter-wave interferometry
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09917-9

Quantum interference of sodium nanoparticles, which can each contain more than 7,000 atoms at masses greater than 170,000 Da, is demonstrated.
item.dc:title: Probing quantum mechanics with nanoparticle matter-wave interferometry
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Pedalino
Bruno E. Ramírez-Galindo
Richard Ferstl
Klaus Hornberger
Markus Arndt
Stefan Gerlich

item.title: Fibre integrated circuits by a multilayered spiral architecture
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09974-0

Fibre integrated circuits created by first preparing circuitry on extremely thin and flexible substrates and then mechanically rolling them up demonstrate microdevice density reaching 100,000 transistors per centimetre and excellent stability under harsh service conditions.
item.dc:title: Fibre integrated circuits by a multilayered spiral architecture
item.dc:creator: Zhen Wang
Ke Chen
Xiang Shi
Qinhao Du

item.title: Quantum effect observed for biggest objects yet
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-04097-y

An experiment has broken the size record for an object exhibiting matter-wave interference — fuelling the debate about possible size constraints.
item.dc:title: Quantum effect observed for biggest objects yet
item.dc:creator: Tim Kovachy

item.title: Schrödinger’s cat just got bigger: quantum physicists create largest ever ‘superposition’
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00177-9

Record-breaking experiment shows that a cluster of thousands of atoms can act like a wave as well as a particle.
item.dc:title: Schrödinger’s cat just got bigger: quantum physicists create largest ever ‘superposition’
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Gibney
